classified.subject: Autoresearch: The feedback loop behind self-improving agents
classified.rationale: Core concept for AI agent architecture — self-improving feedback loops are a key emerging pattern. Directly maps to wiki's agent-focused scope.

classified.subject: How Cursor deploys AI inside the enterprise
classified.rationale: Cursor is a leading AI coding agent product. Enterprise deployment strategy (forward-deployed engineers model) is a significant industry pattern worth documenting.

classified.subject: Warp CEO Zach Lloyd on why software factories are the next phase of coding
classified.rationale: Software factories concept extends AI coding agents into production-scale workflows. Warp is an emerging terminal/AI product. Zach Lloyd (ex-Google) is a relevant voice.

classified.subject: 🎭 Fable 5 Is Back, Sonnet 5 Lands With a Caveat
classified.rationale: New model releases (Sonnet 5, Fable 5) are high-signal events for the wiki. Requires verification of actual release details before creating entity pages.

classified.subject: AIEWF Daily Dispatch: Autoresearch and the tension between AI and human agency
classified.rationale: Follows up on autoresearch concept with agency dimension. AIEWF (AI Engineer World's Fair) is a key conference. Builds on the critical autoresearch newsletter.

classified.subject: 🔬 The Coolest Diffusion Research Isn't in LLMs — Evan Feinberg & Sergey Edunov, Genesis Molecular AI
classified.rationale: Diffusion research applied to molecular AI is tangentially related to the wiki's LLM/Agent focus. Lower priority unless the wiki expands to cover broader AI/ML domains.

key_themes: Self-improving AI agents (autoresearch) — 2 newsletters from swyx on consecutive days
AI coding agents in enterprise (Cursor forward-deployed engineers, Warp software factories)
AI agency and human-AI interaction tensions
